study_fit: Revised schema for RQ2-style analysis with attempt-level and round-level cost structure.

schema.attempts: Per-attempt trajectory with score, unresolved bottleneck, token usage, and cost decomposition.
schema.round_labels: Per-round labeling with attempt index, within-attempt round index, lifecycle stage, mechanism label, and necessity assessment.
schema.cost_summary: Top-level aggregates for first-attempt cost, retry cost, and token inefficiency splits.

task_outcome.final_notes: The agent eventually achieved correct storage and perfect cross-session retrieval, but never produced the required same-session recall behavior in the judged interaction.

inefficiency_summary.interpretation: Most cost did not come from the final cheap fix. It came from a mix of first-attempt gateway/tooling detours and later retries that kept optimizing the wrong bottleneck.

task_summary.note: This should have been a short store-and-recall task, but the run spent most of its budget on gateway/session troubleshooting and later on proving cross-session behavior while the remaining blocker was same-session recall.
task_summary.evidence: By attempt 4 the run had already reached memory_storage=1.0 and cross_session_retrieval=1.0, yet attempts 4-6 still failed because same_session_recall stayed at 0.0.

attempts.remaining_bottleneck: The run never reached a stable judged solution because it got trapped in workspace discovery and gateway/session troubleshooting.
attempts.notes: Grading failed after a long first attempt dominated by infrastructure detours.

attempts.remaining_bottleneck: Storage path was still wrong and no same-session or cross-session recall had been demonstrated.
attempts.notes: The run made genuine storage progress, but almost the entire attempt cost was inherited from a large wasted opening action.

attempts.remaining_bottleneck: The agent fixed the required storage path but still did not satisfy same-session recall and did not complete cross-session retrieval cleanly.
attempts.notes: This was the only retry where most spend directly advanced the task.

attempts.remaining_bottleneck: The only substantive missing criterion was same-session recall, but the attempt kept investing in cross-session proof and verification.
attempts.notes: Performance jumped, but cost efficiency collapsed because the run optimized the wrong remaining target.

attempts.remaining_bottleneck: No bottleneck changed; same-session recall was still absent.
attempts.notes: A very expensive redundant retry with no measurable task improvement.

attempts.remaining_bottleneck: The final cheap answer resembled same-session recall, but it was not credited as the required judged behavior.
attempts.notes: Cheap final patch attempt, but too late and not aligned with what the validator expected.

round_labels.note: Started with workspace and task discovery instead of executing the direct memory workflow.
round_labels.evidence: The run opened with `ls -la` and workspace probing.
The agent listed files and searched for hidden prompt/context files.
`sessions_list` returned a gateway 1006 error.

round_labels.note: Moved into gateway diagnosis instead of task completion.
round_labels.evidence: The agent checked gateway status after the session tool failed.
The run explored gateway help and run options.

round_labels.note: Tried to start the gateway to recover broken session tooling.
round_labels.evidence: The trace shows `openclaw gateway run` launched in background.
The same failing session path was attempted again.

round_labels.note: More process-level debugging with no task progress.
round_labels.evidence: The run polled the gateway background process for logs.
The agent read local state files to infer the multi-session task requirements.
This started the first storage sequence.
The stored facts were later judged accurate.
The task had progress, but the required path was still unresolved.

round_labels.note: Large expensive detour into broken session infrastructure.
round_labels.evidence: This spike followed repeated attempts to inspect sessions/history through failing gateway mechanisms.

round_labels.note: Repeated high-cost exploration with little task progress.
round_labels.evidence: The task still lacked direct same-session and cross-session completion.
Storage content remained the substantive path forward.
The trace confirms continued validation of stored memory.

round_labels.note: Another very expensive troubleshooting spike at the start of the retry.
round_labels.evidence: The run was still trying to recover session/gateway paths instead of focusing on the validator-localized issues.
This falls in the sequence that produced valid memory persistence.
Stored facts later scored well on automated checks.
The path/content distinction was the main remaining storage issue.

round_labels.note: Started fixing defects surfaced by feedback.
round_labels.evidence: Validator notes indicated storage worked but used the wrong path.

round_labels.note: Checked whether the repair addressed the failure.
round_labels.evidence: Subsequent attempts improved the storage-path criterion.
The task was moving from raw storage toward compliant storage.
The run was close to resolving storage-path compliance.

round_labels.note: Critical path repair finally succeeded, though it was expensive within a long retry history.
round_labels.evidence: Validator later confirmed `memory/MEMORY.md` existed with all 5 facts.
After this phase, memory_storage reached 1.0.

round_labels.note: Shifted focus to proving cross-session behavior instead of satisfying the missing same-session criterion.
round_labels.evidence: Feedback had already localized the remaining requirement away from storage.

round_labels.note: Verification effort targeted the wrong remaining bottleneck.
round_labels.evidence: The remaining unresolved criterion was same_session_recall, not storage.

round_labels.note: Continued validating retrieval flow rather than directly answering the same-session questions.
round_labels.evidence: Validator kept same_session_recall at 0.0.
This contributed to later perfect cross-session retrieval.
Cross-session retrieval was eventually scored 1.0.

round_labels.note: Additional setup that did not close the missing criterion.
round_labels.evidence: The task still lacked explicit same-session conversational recall.

round_labels.note: More checking around the wrong issue.
round_labels.evidence: The run kept strengthening already-good retrieval instead of answering Session 2.

round_labels.note: Low-yield verification while the core missing criterion remained unchanged.
round_labels.evidence: The breakdown still showed same_session_recall = 0.0.
This helped achieve accuracy_of_facts = 1.0.

round_labels.note: Very expensive late verification push concentrated on cross-session proof.
round_labels.evidence: This was the largest late-stage spike, yet the unresolved same-session criterion remained unaddressed.

round_labels.note: Another costly late retry with limited incremental value.
round_labels.evidence: Cross-session retrieval already worked, but the retry still did not fix same_session_recall.
The final stdout contains a same-session-style answer, but the judged task still failed to credit the required Session 2 behavior.
